## The Foundation - Ice & Instinct, from $650
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/offerings/foundation/

← Offerings

### The Foundation.

For those who understand that elegance lies in simplicity. The most intimate way to experience the Ice & Instinct standard - delivered by a bartender from my own circle: hand-picked, trusted, and someone I personally stand behind.
From $650 USD Up to 15 guests · 3 hours · New York Metropolitan Area Inquire

I · Overview

#### Distillation, not compromise.

For those who understand that elegance lies in simplicity. The Essential delivers the core of what makes a gathering memorable: impeccable drinks, professional presence, and seamless execution.
This is not a compromise. This is distillation - the same philosophy, the same standards, focused into a refined format designed for intimate celebrations.
A bartender I know and trust personally arrives with the tools, technique, and presence to transform your home bar into something more. Classic cocktails executed with precision. Contemporary favorites crafted with care. Every drink made properly, every guest attended to.

This is not a staffing service. This is hospitality with intention.
Your guests experience the difference from the first pour.

II · The Menu Protocol Curated Simplicity. The Foundation of Excellence.

#### Curated simplicity.

Prior to your event, we collaborate on a menu of 4-6 signature cocktails. Classics perfected. Crowd favorites refined. Each drink selected to balance variety, flow, and your available spirits.

II · The Focus

#### The focus.

Flawless technique on timeless recipes. Proper dilution, balanced flavors, appropriate glassware, and garnishes that matter. The fundamentals, executed without shortcuts.

III · Scalability

#### Scalability & Investment.

Calibrated for intimate gatherings where quality remains uncompromised regardless of guest count. A bartender from my own circle delivers the Ice & Instinct standard - someone I know personally, hand-picked, and vouch for.

A The Intimate Up to 15 guests Personnel 1 Professional Bartender Focus Attentive service, consistent quality, personal engagement From $650

IV · The Experience

#### What's Included.

The specific elements of your experience.

- 01

##### Pre-Event Consultation

A focused conversation to understand your occasion, guest count, preferences, and available spirits. Menu finalized 5-7 days prior.

- 02

##### Professional Bar Setup

Your bartender arrives with a complete toolkit: shakers, strainers, jiggers, bar spoons, muddlers, and essential garnish prep equipment.

- 03

##### Ice Coordination

Guidance on appropriate ice quantities and formats for your event. Standard ice may be sourced by the host, or premium ice options arranged through Ice & Instinct at additional cost.

- 04

##### 3 Hours of Professional Service

Arrival 30 minutes prior for setup. Three hours of active service. Additional hours available at $75 per hour.

- 05

##### Station Restoration

Upon conclusion, your bar area is returned to order. Glassware rinsed, surfaces wiped, workspace organized.

V · The Foundation

#### Standard Inclusions.

Every Ice & Instinct experience, regardless of tier, includes the foundation of our craft.
01

##### Trained Professional

A bartender from my own circle - someone I know personally and stand behind, skilled in proper technique and professional hospitality.

02

##### Guest Engagement

Friendly, professional interaction. Drink recommendations tailored to individual preferences. The warmth of genuine hospitality.

03

##### Professional Tooling

A complete bar kit with quality tools. No plastic pourers. No shortcuts.

04

##### Setup & Breakdown

Complete organization of the working area before and after service.

VI · The Canvas

#### What the Host Provides.

To ensure a seamless experience, the host provides the following canvas.

- 01

##### Spirits & Mixers

All alcohol, mixers, juices, and sodas for the agreed menu. A detailed shopping list is provided during consultation.

- 02

##### Glassware

Appropriate glasses for the selected cocktails. Guidance provided. Rental coordination available upon request via The Curator add-on.

- 03

##### Ice

Sufficient ice for the duration of service. Quantity guidance provided based on guest count and menu.

- 04

##### Workspace

A stable surface for bar setup, access to running water, and adequate lighting.

#### Experience notes.

- The Foundation is the most intimate way into the Ice & Instinct standard. It is not a reduction of quality - it is a focused application of our principles.

- For those seeking deeper customization, signature cocktail development, or the personal involvement of the Master Mixologist, we invite you to explore Simplicity
, Bespoke
, or Omakase
.

- Foundation is where many first experience the standard - and return for the evenings that follow.

## Offerings - Ice & Instinct
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/offerings/

### Four distinct levels. One standard.

From the purity of a classic cocktail to the theatre of molecular improvisation. Choose the rhythm that fits your evening.

i. Foundation

##### The Foundation

From $650 · up to 15 guests · 3 hours Impeccable drinks, seamless service. No shortcuts, only intention. The standard from which every other level departs.
Explore →

ii. Simplicity

##### Perfection in Simplicity

From $900 · up to 12 guests · 4 hours Timeless cocktails executed with quiet precision. A masterful solo performance for those who value craft over theatre.
Explore →

iii. Bespoke

##### Bespoke Design & Artistry

From $1,800 · up to 30 guests · 4 hours Signature cocktails tailored to your event's theme and vision. Your story, crafted into every glass.
Explore →

iv. Omakase

##### Omakase Improvisation

From $3,000 · up to 25 guests · open No menu. No repetition. Real-time creation in dialogue with the room. Complete trust. Unrepeatable moments.
Explore →

04 Tiers Explore each tier

One alchemist. A team when the room calls for it.

#### Built to scale.

Any tier scales with a full bar team for larger events - weddings, galas, and corporate gatherings - quoted on request.

The fee
Covers the craft, the service, and the expertise.

Spirits & wine
Provided by you, or sourced on your behalf at supplier cost.

Ice, glassware & rentals
Arranged through the Concierge, billed at supplier cost.

#### Before you ask.

The honest answers, plainly given.

- What is Ice and Instinct? A private mixology studio for the home and the event. Led by one alchemist and carried by a team of trusted New York bartenders, it brings the craft, theatre, and discipline of a world-class bar into your own room.

- What actually happens at an event? We arrive, build the bar, and run the evening end to end. Each cocktail is made in real time, with intention. You set the rhythm through one of four tiers, from impeccable classics to off-menu omakase improvisation.

- Is alcohol included in the price? No. The tier price covers the craft, service, and expertise. You may provide the spirits and wine yourself, or we handle the entire purchase on your behalf at supplier cost.

- Do you bring everything - bar, tools, ice, glassware? Yes. The bar setup, tools, and service are fully handled. Specialty ice from Michelin-level suppliers, curated glassware, and rentals are arranged through the Concierge and billed at supplier cost.

- Where do you serve? The New York metropolitan area and New Jersey. Events further out are welcome, and any travel is included in your private quote.

- Can you handle a large event or wedding? Yes. One alchemist leads, and a team of trusted New York bartenders scales the service from an intimate solo performance to a full bar team for weddings, galas, and corporate events.

- Which tier should I choose? It depends on the room and the evening you have in mind. Foundation is for intimate gatherings, Simplicity for a refined solo performance, Bespoke for signature cocktails built around your theme, and Omakase for a no-menu, improvised experience. Tell us the date, guest count, and mood, and we will guide you.

- Can the cocktails be tailored, and do you offer non-alcoholic options? Yes to both. Every menu is built around your taste, theme, and any dietary needs, and a full zero-proof program is available so each guest is served with the same care.

- How far ahead should I book? Two or more weeks is ideal, and shorter timelines are often possible. Ask, and we will tell you honestly what we can do.

- How does the deposit work? A flat $500 deposit reserves your date. The balance is due before the event.

- What is your cancellation policy? More than 14 days before the event, your $500 deposit is refunded. Within 14 days, the deposit is non-refundable but transferable to a future date within 12 months, subject to availability. Within 48 hours of the event, the full booking value is due.

- How do I book? Send the date, the room, and the guest count through the inquiry form. You will have a private quote within one business day.

#### Choose the rhythm.

Tell us about the evening you have in mind. We respond within one business day with a private quote.
Request a private quote →
Meet the Alchemist

By appointment only · Est. 2024

---

## Omakase Improvisation - Ice & Instinct, from $3,000
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/offerings/omakase/

← Offerings

### Omakase Improvisation.

Pure creation. No menu. Unrepeatable moments. Complete trust. Cocktails created spontaneously, in real time, for each guest.
From $3,000 USD Up to 25 guests · 4-6 hours · New York Metropolitan Area Inquire

I · Overview

#### Performance art.

For the true connoisseur who understands that the highest form of craft is improvisation. This is omakase for cocktails - a ritual reserved for those who collect spirits, cultivate home bars, and seek experiences that cannot be replicated.
There is no menu. No predetermined recipes. Only mastery, intuition, and the alchemy that happens when experience meets the unexpected.
Your private mixologist arrives with a quarter-century of global hospitality expertise. Each cocktail is created spontaneously, in real time, tailored to individual guests' moods, energy, and palates. You witness cocktails being born - techniques you've never seen, flavor structures that challenge assumptions.

This is not bartending. This is performance art.
Guests do not observe - they participate in the creation itself.

II · The Menu Protocol Pure Improvisation. The Highest Form of Trust.

#### The Selection.

We use your existing spirits, your collection, and the energy of the room as our canvas. No recipe cards, no pre-event design. Cocktails are created spontaneously for each guest, based on real-time dialogue, intuition, and the resources available in your bar.

II · The Focus

#### Advanced technique.

Smoke infusion, fat washing, rapid infusion, temperature layering, molecular garnish - methods reserved for world-class bars, executed intimately in your home.

III · Scalability

#### Scalability & Investment.

The Alchemist experience is intentionally limited to preserve the focus required for spontaneous, omakase-style creation. The Master Mixologist is the sole creative force. A dedicated logistical assistant is present to preserve focus, flow, and the integrity of the ritual - their role is strictly operational and never part of the creative act.

A The Soloist Up to 15 guests Personnel 1 Master Mixologist + 1 Logistical Assistant Focus Deep engagement and complex, individual creation From $3,000
B The Ensemble 16 to 25 guests · hard limit Personnel 1 Master Mixologist + 1 Shadow (logistical) Focus Critical logistical support so the Master remains fully immersed in improvisation From $3,500
C Beyond Limits Over 25 guests Personnel Custom inquiry only Focus This scale compromises the core omakase experience Not recommended

IV · The Experience

#### What's Included.

The specific milestones of your evening, from first consultation to final pour.

- 01

##### Pre-Event Discovery Session

Two to three weeks prior, we conduct a comprehensive consultation and home bar assessment. This is not menu planning - it is understanding the canvas before the performance begins.

- 02

##### The Alchemist's Kit

I arrive with specialty technique equipment, rare ingredients, proprietary tinctures, and unique garnishes not available commercially.

- 03

##### The Ice Ritual mandatory inclusion

Ice selection is determined exclusively by the Master Mixologist. All ice used for this experience is professionally sourced, ordered, and quality-controlled by Ice & Instinct. The host covers the cost of ice as a separate line item, finalized during the consultation. For optimal tempering and performance readiness, ice delivery is scheduled to arrive prior to the Master Mixologist’s arrival. Ice quality is non-negotiable and cannot be substituted, supplied, or sourced by the host.

- 04

##### 4-6 Hours of Improvisational Service

Flexible service duration, guided by the rhythm of the evening. Every cocktail is a singular event.

- 05

##### Station Restoration

Upon conclusion, your space is returned to order.

V · The Foundation

#### Standard Inclusions.

Every Ice & Instinct experience, regardless of tier, includes the foundation of our craft.
01

##### Private Master Mixologist

A dedicated professional with a quarter-century of global hospitality expertise.

02

##### Educational Engagement

Conversations about terroir, fermentation, technique, and cocktail history emerge naturally. Your gathering becomes a living masterclass.

03

##### Professional Tooling

A compact case of gold-standard mixology tools, plus advanced technique equipment.

04

##### Setup & Breakdown

Complete organization of the immediate working area.

VI · The Canvas

#### What the Host Provides.

To enable pure improvisation, the host provides the canvas.

- 01

##### Existing Spirits & Inventory

The host's spirit collection and home bar inventory form the primary palette for the evening.

- 02

##### Curated Glassware Collection

This experience assumes thoughtfully selected, high-quality glassware. Large-format stemware, sculptural coupes, elegant rocks glasses, and varied vessels allow each creation to fully express its character. The vessel is not an accessory - it is part of the performance.

- 03

##### Infrastructure

A suitable work surface with adequate space for technique work, access to running water, and electricity.

- 04

##### Openness to the Unexpected

Complete trust in the Master Mixologist's spontaneous creative vision.

#### Experience notes.

- The Alchemist experience is intentionally designed as a focused, solo improvisational performance. The Master Mixologist remains the sole creative force throughout the evening.

- Operational support is scaled internally based on the number of guests to preserve flow, cleanliness, and uninterrupted creative focus. The size and structure of the supporting team are determined exclusively by the mixologist and are included as part of the experience.

- Certain material elements required to execute the ritual at the highest level - such as professional ice formats, specialty glassware, or select atmospheric components - may be arranged separately through the Concierge
. These elements support the performance without altering its nature.

- When the energy of the room calls for it, the experience may continue beyond its initial frame as a natural continuation of the ritual itself.

Ice & Instinct / By appointment

#### Surrender to improvisation.

Tell us the date, the room, and your spirits collection.
We return with a tailored consultation framework within one business day.
Every evening includes professional clear ice and temperature, sourced at supplier cost.
Reserve your evening
Add enhancements

A flat $500 deposit reserves your date - fully refundable until 14 days before the evening.

## Perfection in Simplicity - Ice & Instinct, from $900
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/offerings/simplicity/

← Offerings

### Perfection in Simplicity.

For the host who values heritage over hype. The world's most iconic cocktails executed with surgical precision - by the Master Mixologist personally.
From $900 USD Up to 12 guests · 4 hours · New York Metropolitan Area Inquire

I · Overview

#### Heritage over hype.

For the host who values heritage over hype. Your gathering deserves cocktails balanced to absolute perfection - no flourish, no theater, only pure mastery.
This is the Executive Standard: a service designed for those who understand that true luxury whispers. Whether for an intimate board dinner, a family celebration, or a high-level reception, we deliver the world’s most iconic cocktails with surgical precision. We do not invent here; we execute the legends.
Your private mixologist arrives with a compact case of professional tools and a quarter-century of global hospitality experience distilled into seamless service. You enjoy the company of your guests; we ensure the glass in their hand is flawless.

True luxury whispers.
We do not invent. We execute the legends.

II · The Menu Protocol Classics Only. Strictly defined. Flawlessly executed.

#### The selection.

Prior to the event, we curate a menu of 3-4 timeless classics (e.g., The Martini, The Old Fashioned, The Negroni, The Daiquiri).

II · The Focus

#### The focus.

This package does not include signature cocktail design or experimental mixology. We agree on the classics, and we perfect them using superior spirits, precise dilution, and temperature control.

III · Scalability

#### Scalability & Investment.

Service capacity is strictly calibrated to ensure the Purist standard of execution is never compromised, regardless of guest count.

A The Intimate Up to 12 guests Personnel 1 Master Mixologist (solo performance) Focus Quiet, attentive precision. Direct guest interaction. From $900

IV · The Experience

#### What's Included.

The specific milestones of your experience.

- 01

##### The Classics Consultation

One week prior, we finalize your selection of classics and set the tone for the evening.

- 02

##### The Procurement Specification

You receive a precise, architectural list of spirits, botanicals, and ice formats tailored to your menu. We provide the blueprint; you retain control of purchasing.

- 03

##### Four Hours of Execution

We arrive 30 minutes early for setup. Service is continuous, quiet, and unobtrusive.

- 04

##### Station Restoration

Upon conclusion, the bar station is returned to its pristine condition. We manage all bar-specific waste (citrus, herbs, melting ice) within the service perimeter.

V · The Foundation

#### Standard Inclusions.

Every Ice & Instinct experience, regardless of tier, includes the foundation of our craft.
01

##### Private Master Mixologist

A dedicated professional with a quarter-century of global hospitality expertise.

02

##### Professional Tooling

A compact, aesthetic case of gold-standard mixology tools (shakers, jiggers, strainers). No bulky equipment, no portable bars.

03

##### On-Site Ritual Performance

The visual elegance of proper stirring, shaking, and pouring.

04

##### Setup & Breakdown

Complete organization of the immediate working area.

VI · The Canvas

#### What the Host Provides.

To ensure the integrity of the service, the host provides the following canvas.

- 01

##### Spirits, Mixers & Garnishes

All alcohol and fresh ingredients must be on-site prior to arrival, based on the provided Specification.

- 02

##### Glassware

Proper vessels for the selected cocktails (or rental via The Curator add-on).

- 03

##### Infrastructure

A dedicated work surface (kitchen counter, dining table, or existing bar), access to running water, and electricity.

- 04

##### Ice Supply

Quantity and formats (cubes, spheres, spears) as specified in the consultation.

- 05

##### Floor Service Boundary

Ice & Instinct focuses exclusively on production within the bar perimeter. For passing drinks, clearing glassware from the room, or general venue tidying, separate floor staff is required.

#### Experience extensions.

- The Executive Standard is designed as a complete and self-contained experience. In certain cases, the scope of service may be thoughtfully adjusted to meet the specific needs of the gathering.

- A selection of additional services - including logistical coordination, glassware sourcing, specialty ice, and presentation support - is available on the Concierge
page.

- When the flow of the evening naturally calls for it, the Master Mixologist's presence may continue beyond the initial service window. Such considerations are addressed discreetly and without interrupting the integrity of the experience.

Ice & Instinct / By appointment

#### Strip away the unnecessary.

## Privacy Policy - Ice & Instinct
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/privacy/

### Privacy Policy

Last updated · June 8, 2026

#### 1. Who we are

Ice & Instinct ("we", "us", "our") is a private mixology and at-home bartending service operated by Teimuraz Benidze , serving the New York metropolitan area. This policy explains what information we collect when you visit www.iceinstinct.com
or send us an inquiry, how we use it, and the choices you have. We are the data controller for that information.

#### 2. Information we collect

- Inquiry and booking details. When you request a consultation through our scheduling provider (YouCanBook.me) or by email, we receive the details you provide: your name, email address, and any event details you share (date, location, guest count, preferences). These reach our private inbox.

- Analytics data. We use Google Analytics 4 to understand how the site is used (pages viewed, approximate location, device and browser type, and on-site actions). Google Analytics sets cookies (for example _ga ) and processes a truncated version of your IP address. See our Cookie Policy
for the full list and how to opt out.

- Server logs. Our hosting provider records standard technical logs (IP address, user-agent, requested URL, timestamp) for security, performance, and abuse prevention.

#### 3. How we use your information

- To respond to your inquiry, prepare a quote, and deliver the service you book.

- To send event-related communication through the channel you specified.

- To understand and improve how the site performs (analytics, in aggregate).

- To keep the site secure and prevent abuse.

- To meet legal, tax, and accounting obligations.

We do not sell or rent your personal information, and we do not share it with advertisers or use it for marketing unrelated to your inquiry.

#### 4. Cookies and analytics

The site uses a small number of cookies, primarily for analytics. We do not run advertising or cross-site tracking cookies. You can control or refuse cookies in your browser, and you can opt out of Google Analytics using Google's browser add-on. Full detail is in our Cookie Policy
.

#### 5. Service providers we share data with

- YouCanBook.me - consultation scheduling and inquiry intake.

- Google Analytics (Google LLC) - website usage measurement.

- Google Fonts - typeface delivery.

- Hostinger - website hosting and server logs.

Each provider processes data only to deliver its service to us, under its own privacy terms.

#### 6. How long we keep your data

Inquiry submissions and email correspondence are kept for as long as needed to deliver the service you booked, plus a reasonable period afterward for accounting and legal records. Analytics data is retained for up to 14 months. You may request deletion at any time, subject to records we must keep by law.

#### 7. Your rights

Depending on where you live, you may have the right to access, correct, delete, or port your personal information, and to opt out of certain processing. New York residents may ask what information we hold and request its deletion. California residents have rights under the CCPA/CPRA, including the right to know, delete, and opt out of "sale" or "sharing" (we do neither). EU/UK residents have rights under the GDPR/UK GDPR. To exercise any right, write to us via the contact page
; we will not discriminate against you for doing so.

#### 8. Age

This site and our services are intended for adults of legal drinking age (21 or older in the United States). We do not knowingly collect information from anyone under 18. See our Responsible Service & Alcohol Policy
.

#### 9. Security

We take reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your information. No method of transmission or storage is completely secure, so we cannot guarantee absolute security.

#### 10. Changes

We may update this policy from time to time. The "Last updated" date above reflects the most recent change.

#### 11. Contact

For privacy questions or data requests, reach us via the contact page
.

Ice & Instinct / Legal

#### Questions? Answered plainly.

Write to us about anything on this page. A personal reply within one business day.
Begin the conversation

By appointment only New York Metropolitan Area Est. 2024

---

## Responsible Service & Alcohol Policy - Ice & Instinct
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/responsible-service/

### Responsible Service & Alcohol Policy

Last updated · June 8, 2026

#### 1. Purpose

Ice & Instinct provides private bartending and mixology service in the New York metropolitan area. The enjoyment of cocktails depends on serving them responsibly. This policy explains how we serve, the legal framework we serve within, and the responsibilities shared between us and the host. It works alongside our Terms of Service
.

#### 2. Legal drinking age (21+)

We serve alcohol only to guests who are 21 years of age or older , the legal drinking age in the United States. Our bartenders may ask any guest for valid government-issued photo identification and will decline service to anyone who cannot establish that they are 21 or older. We never knowingly serve a minor.

#### 3. We serve; we do not sell alcohol

We provide bartending labor and craft, not the retail sale of alcohol. The alcohol served at your event is supplied by you as the host, or sourced on your behalf at supplier cost with no markup on the alcohol. Because we do not sell alcohol, we operate as a service provider rather than a licensed retailer; where a venue or event requires a permit to serve alcohol, securing it is the host's or venue's responsibility.

#### 4. How we serve responsibly

- We pace service and pour to standard measures rather than over-pour.

- We make water available and encourage guests to drink it alongside cocktails.

- We watch for signs of intoxication and adjust or stop service accordingly.

- We offer and prepare quality non-alcoholic options on request.

- Our service follows responsible-beverage-service practices.

#### 5. Refusing and stopping service

We reserve the absolute right, without liability and without refund, to refuse or stop serving any guest who is, or appears to be, under 21, unable to show valid identification, or intoxicated. We may also end an engagement where guest conduct or circumstances make safe, lawful service impossible. Please support our bartenders when they make these calls; they make them to keep your guests safe.

#### 6. New York dram shop and social host law

New York law, including the Dram Shop Act and the social host provisions of the General Obligations Law (sections 11-100 and 11-101), can impose liability on a person who unlawfully provides alcohol to a minor or to a visibly intoxicated person who then causes injury. As the host who supplies the alcohol and invites the guests, you retain the responsibilities the law places on a host. Our responsible-service practices reduce risk but do not transfer or remove a host's legal responsibilities.

#### 7. Host responsibilities

- Confirm that every guest to be served is 21 or older.

- Supply alcohol lawfully and secure any permit the venue or event requires.

- Arrange safe transportation for any guest who should not drive, and never pressure anyone to drink.

- Support our bartenders when they pace, refuse, or stop service.

#### 8. Safe transportation

We encourage every host to plan ahead for rides home: rideshare, designated drivers, or car service. No guest should drive after drinking.

#### 9. Liability and indemnification

Our responsibilities and the limits of our liability, along with the host's indemnification obligations, are set out in our Terms of Service
. Nothing in this policy waives any duty that the law does not permit to be waived.

#### 10. Contact

Questions about how we serve? Reach us via the contact page
.

Ice & Instinct / Legal

#### Questions? Answered plainly.

Write to us about anything on this page. A personal reply within one business day.
Begin the conversation

By appointment only New York Metropolitan Area Est. 2024

---

## Terms of Service - Ice & Instinct
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/terms/

### Terms of Service

Last updated · June 8, 2026

#### 1. Who we are

Ice & Instinct is a private mixology and at-home bartending service operated by Teimuraz Benidze , serving the New York metropolitan area. These Terms govern your use of www.iceinstinct.com
and the engagement between us and any client who books a service through our scheduling page or by direct agreement. By inquiring or booking, you agree to these Terms and to our Responsible Service & Alcohol Policy
.

#### 2. Our service model

We provide professional bartending and mixology labor and service , not the sale of alcohol. We do not sell, resell, or furnish alcoholic beverages for a fee. Alcohol is supplied by you, the client/host, or sourced on your behalf at supplier cost as a convenience with no markup on the alcohol itself. Because we do not sell alcohol, our bartending service operates without a retail liquor license; where any event or venue requires a permit to serve alcohol, obtaining it is the client's or venue's responsibility.

#### 3. Services we offer

Private mixology service in the New York metropolitan area, organised in tiers:

- The Foundation - from $650 USD, up to 15 guests, 3 hours.

- Perfection in Simplicity - from $900 USD, up to 12 guests, 4 hours.

- Bespoke Design & Artistry - from $1,800 USD, up to 30 guests, 4 hours.

- Omakase Improvisation - from $3,000 USD, up to 25 guests, open hours.

- Private Concierge - bespoke pricing.

Each engagement is confirmed by a private quote sent in response to your inquiry. The confirmed quote is the binding scope.

#### 4. Pricing & payment

Pricing is bespoke per event and confirmed in writing. A flat $500 deposit reserves your date; the remaining balance is due before the event, unless otherwise agreed. Currency is USD.

#### 5. Cancellation

Cancellation more than 14 days before the event: full deposit refunded. Within 14 days: deposit non-refundable but transferable to a future date within 12 months, subject to availability. Within 48 hours: full booking value due. We may cancel or end service, without refund, where service cannot proceed safely or lawfully (see Section 7).

#### 6. What's included

Each tier includes the items listed at the time of quote: bartender service, tools, bar setup, and breakdown. Spirits and wine are provided by you or sourced on your behalf at supplier cost. Specialty ice, curated glassware, additional staff, and add-on services are arranged through the Concierge and billed separately at supplier cost.

#### 7. Responsible service and age

All guests served must be of legal drinking age (21 or older). We serve in accordance with our Responsible Service & Alcohol Policy
and reserve the absolute right, without liability and without refund, to:

- refuse or stop service to any guest who is, or appears to be, under 21 or unable to show valid ID;

- refuse or stop service to any guest who is, or appears to be, intoxicated; and

- end the engagement where guest conduct, the venue, or circumstances make safe, lawful service impossible.

#### 8. Client and host responsibilities

- Provide accurate event information at the time of inquiry.

- Ensure the venue is accessible for setup at least 90 minutes before service.

- Confirm that every guest to be served is 21 or older, and assist with ID checks on request.

- Lawfully supply the alcohol to be served, and obtain any permit the venue or event requires.

- Act as the host of the gathering. As host, you are responsible for your guests and for arranging safe transportation for anyone who should not drive.

#### 9. Assumption of risk & host liability

The consumption of alcohol carries inherent risks. Under New York law, including the Dram Shop Act and social host provisions (General Obligations Law sections 11-100 and 11-101), a person who unlawfully furnishes alcohol to a minor or to a visibly intoxicated person may be held liable for resulting harm. You acknowledge that, as the host who supplies the alcohol and invites the guests, you retain the responsibilities the law places on a host, and that our role is limited to providing responsible bartending service.

#### 10. Limitation of liability

We provide service in good faith and to a professional standard. To the fullest extent permitted by law, our total liability for any claim arising from a service is limited to the fees you paid us for that service, and we are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. Nothing in these Terms limits liability that cannot be limited by law.

#### 11. Indemnification

To the fullest extent permitted by law, you agree to indemnify and hold us harmless from claims, damages, and costs arising from: guest conduct; a guest's intoxication or underage drinking that occurs despite our reasonable refusal of service; the alcohol you supplied; the condition of the venue; or your breach of these Terms.

#### 12. Insurance

We carry commercial general liability insurance for our service. This does not replace the coverage a host or venue should hold. We recommend you confirm that your homeowner's, renter's, or venue policy provides host liquor liability coverage for your event.

#### 13. Intellectual property

Recipes, photography, and brand materials remain the property of Ice & Instinct unless transferred in writing. Custom recipes designed for your event may be shared with you in a printed take-home format on request.

#### 14. Confidentiality

We treat all client information as confidential and do not share guest lists, addresses, or event details with third parties. We do not photograph guests during service without explicit prior consent.

#### 15. Governing law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of New York, USA. Disputes will first be addressed by good-faith discussion; if not resolved, by the state or federal courts located in New York County, New York.

#### 16. Changes

We may update these Terms from time to time. The "Last updated" date above reflects the most recent change. For ongoing engagements, the Terms in force at the time of your booking apply.

#### 17. Contact

Questions about these Terms? Reach us via the contact page
.

Ice & Instinct / Legal

#### Questions? Answered plainly.

Write to us about anything on this page. A personal reply within one business day.
Begin the conversation

By appointment only New York Metropolitan Area Est. 2024

---

## Wedding Bar & Cocktail Service NYC & NJ - Ice & Instinct
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/weddings/

### One day. One standard.

A wedding multiplies everything - the guests, the pace, the weight of a single glass. The bar should hold. We bring the same craft that runs our private evenings, carried by a full professional team, so the two hundredth pour tastes like the first.
From $650 USD Any tier scales with a full bar team · NYC Metro & New Jersey Inquire for your date

I · The Scaled Team

#### One standard, multiplied.

Every Ice & Instinct tier scales for a wedding - from The Foundation at $650 to Omakase Improvisation. The lead does not change: Teimuraz Benidze, certified sommelier, a quarter-century in hospitality. Around him, a team of trusted New York bartenders, briefed on your menu, your timeline, and your standard.
Staffing follows the room, not a formula sheet: roughly one bartender per 40 to 50 guests, quoted per event. A ceremony of sixty and a reception of two hundred are different evenings, and they are priced as such - precisely, in writing, before you commit. What does not change is the craft: professional clear ice as standard, real technique at every station, and a service rhythm set so the bar never becomes the queue your guests remember.

The evening we pour for twelve, held steady for two hundred.
Staffing ratios are confirmed in your written quote.

II · His & Hers

#### Two signatures, one story.

Through the Bespoke Design & Artistry flow, two signature cocktails are designed around the couple - one for each of you, or one for each chapter of the story. Menu cards are included.

- 01

##### The conversation

Before any recipe, we listen. How you met, what you drink, what you refuse to drink, the place or season the marriage belongs to. The brief comes from the couple, not a template.

- 02

##### The design

Two signatures are composed - structure, glassware, garnish, and name considered as one. Each drink should be unmistakably yours, and still hold its own against any classic on the menu.

- 03

##### The menu cards

Menu cards are included - the two signatures, named and described, set at the bar so guests order them by name all night.

- 04

##### The service

On the day, the signatures lead the menu beside the classics, poured identically at every station. The hundredth is indistinguishable from the first.

III · Zero-Proof

#### The same craft, without the proof.

A full non-alcoholic track runs beside the main menu - for the guests who do not drink, and for the ones simply pacing a long day.
01

##### A full program, not a token

A complete zero-proof menu stands beside the cocktail list - composed drinks with structure and finish, not a pitcher of something sweet.

02

##### Clear ice, real technique

The zero-proof track is built with the same professional clear ice, the same tools, and the same technique as everything else at the bar.

03

##### Served without ceremony

No separate table, no lesser glassware, no quiet downgrade. A guest holding a zero-proof drink looks like every other guest holding a cocktail.

04

##### Designed with the couple

The zero-proof list takes shape in the same consultation as the main menu, so it carries the wedding's palate rather than an afterthought.

IV · For Planners

#### Logistics, in plain terms.

For the professionals running the day - the answers you need before the walkthrough, stated in writing.

- 01

##### Certificate of insurance

A COI is provided on request, named to the venue's requirements.

- 02

##### Arrival and setup

We arrive ahead of service to build and dress the bar. Setup timing is confirmed with you in advance - and held.

- 03

##### Breakdown

At close, the station is broken down and the space returned to order. No morning-after surprises for the venue.

- 04

##### Power and water

Requirements are minimal: a standard outlet and access to running water cover nearly every setup. Anything beyond that is flagged at the walkthrough, not on the day.

- 05

##### Alongside the caterer

The bar runs as its own discipline and coordinates with the caterer and the planner - toasts, dinner service, and last call land where the run-of-show puts them.

- 06

##### New Jersey home base

Based in New Jersey, serving Manhattan, Brooklyn, Westchester, the Hamptons, and Greenwich - without the Manhattan vendor logistics premium.

#### In their own words.

References available on request.

#### Before the toast.

What couples and planners ask, answered plainly.

- How far in advance should we book? As early as your date is fixed. A flat $500 deposit holds the date - fully refundable until 14 days before the evening, transferable for 12 months. Shorter timelines are often possible; ask, and we will tell you honestly what we can do.

- How many guests can you serve? Any tier scales with a full bar team for weddings. Staffing follows the room - roughly one bartender per 40 to 50 guests - and is quoted per event, in writing, before you commit.

- What is included? The bar team, professional tools, and professional clear ice as standard, with setup, service, and breakdown handled end to end. His-and-hers signature cocktails are designed through the Bespoke Design & Artistry flow, with menu cards included.

- Is alcohol included in the price? No. The price covers the craft, service, and expertise. You may provide the spirits and wine yourself, or we handle the entire purchase on your behalf at supplier cost.

- Can you serve guests who do not drink? Yes. A full zero-proof program runs beside the main menu - the same clear ice, the same technique, the same glassware. A guest holding a zero-proof cocktail is never made to feel they were given less.

- Where do you travel? Manhattan, Brooklyn, Westchester, the Hamptons, Greenwich, and North New Jersey. Our New Jersey home base means no Manhattan vendor logistics premium. Events further out are welcome - travel is included in your private quote.

Ice & Instinct / By appointment

#### Reserve the date.

Tell us the date, the venue, and the guest count.
A tailored proposal returns within one business day.
Inquire for your date
Call +1 (917) 292-7859

A flat $500 deposit reserves your date - fully refundable until 14 days before the evening.

By appointment only New York Metropolitan Area & New Jersey Est. 2024

## Private Bartending Westchester & Greenwich - Ice & Instinct
URL: https://www.iceinstinct.com/westchester-greenwich/

### Westchester & Greenwich.

Scarsdale, Rye, Bedford, the backcountry. Private dinners in houses built for entertaining - a bar brought to the house, run quietly, and gone before midnight.
From $650 USD Four tiers · Westchester & Fairfield County · By appointment Inquire for your evening

I · The Towns

#### Houses built for entertaining.

There is a particular kind of evening north of the city. Twelve guests in Scarsdale or Rye, dinner called for seven because the morning still matters, a host who knows exactly what is resting in the cellar and expects the bar to keep up. The room is private, the names at the table are sometimes known, and nothing about the night is meant to travel further than the end of the driveway.
We built our service for precisely that room. Teimuraz Benidze - the first certified sommelier in Georgia's history, holding four sommelier credentials - leads every evening, and the bar is run the way these houses are run: arranged before guests arrive, quiet while it works, gone without a trace. Professional clear ice is standard, thirteen signature cocktails carry the menu, and a full zero-proof program serves the guests pacing a school-night dinner with the same care as everyone else.
The address changes; the standard does not. A wedding
at a Bedford barn carries a full bar team. A client dinner
at a Greenwich office comes with invoicing and a certificate of insurance. And most often, it is simply dinner at the house - which is where this work began.

The evening stays in the room.
Scarsdale · Rye · Bronxville · Bedford · Chappaqua · Armonk · Greenwich backcountry

II · The Evening

#### How the night actually runs.

Not a promise of atmosphere - the sequence itself, from the parkway to the last quiet click of the kitchen door.

- 01

##### The route

We drive in from our New Jersey base with everything the bar needs - up the Hutchinson, off the Merritt, or along the back roads to Bedford. Travel is included in the quote, with no city vendor logistics premium and no van idling in the driveway at dusk.

- 02

##### The setup

The bar is built and dressed before your first guest turns in from the road. A butler's pantry, a corner of the library, the pool house in June, a heated tent in December - we work with the room you give us. A standard outlet and running water cover nearly every setup.

- 03

##### The service

Cocktails are composed at the pace of the dinner, not a queue. A sommelier behind the bar means the host's own cellar is read, not rivaled - your wine is poured and paired with the respect it was collected for.

- 04

##### The close

Dinners here end at a civilized hour, and we are staffed for exactly that. Last call lands where the host wants it, the station is broken down in silence, and the kitchen is returned as found. By the time the last car leaves, so have we.

- 05

##### The discretion

Some of our hosts are known names. Who attended, what was poured, what was said over the second round - none of it travels. No photographs, no tagging, no guest list kept beyond the evening.

III · The Cellar

#### For households with a cellar.

Many houses in this county hold serious cellars. The bar you hire should know what to do with one.
01

##### A sommelier at the bar

Teimuraz Benidze was the first certified sommelier in Georgia's history and holds four sommelier credentials. The person mixing your cocktails can also decant your Burgundy correctly - and knows when to do which.

02

##### Your bottles, respected

Spirits and wine are never folded into the price. You provide them - households with a cellar usually prefer to - or we purchase on your behalf at supplier cost, documented. Either way, what is opened is treated as the privilege it is.

03

##### The holiday season

Thanksgiving through New Year is when this county entertains, and December dates go to the hosts who hold them early. A $500 deposit reserves yours - fully refundable until 14 days out, transferable for 12 months.

04

##### When the occasion grows

The same standard scales past the dinner table: weddings
carry a full bar team, and corporate events
come with invoicing, a W-9, and a COI for the venue.

IV · Investment

#### Four tiers. One standard.

Choose the rhythm that fits the evening. Every tier travels to Westchester and Greenwich at the same from-price - the drive is in the quote, not a surcharge - and any tier scales with a full bar team for larger gatherings. Spirits are not included: the host provides them, or we purchase at supplier cost.

A The Foundation Up to 15 guests · 3 hours From $650
B Perfection in Simplicity Up to 12 guests · 4 hours From $900
C Bespoke Design & Artistry Up to 30 guests · 4 hours From $1,800
D Omakase Improvisation Open-ended From $3,000+
A flat $500 deposit reserves your date - fully refundable until 14 days before the evening.

#### Local questions.

What hosts in Westchester and Greenwich ask, answered plainly.

- Do you travel to Scarsdale, Rye, Bedford, and the Greenwich backcountry? Yes. Westchester and the Greenwich side of Fairfield County are core service area for us, alongside Manhattan and New Jersey. We drive in from our New Jersey base with everything the bar needs - travel is included in your private quote, and there is no city vendor logistics premium.

- What kinds of homes and venues do you set up in? Estate kitchens and butler's pantries, libraries, wine cellars, pool houses, heated tents, club rooms in Rye and Greenwich. The bar needs a standard outlet and access to running water - nearly every house in the county has both where we need them. A certificate of insurance is provided on request for clubs and managed venues.

- Our dinners end early. How does the timing work? We build the bar before your first guest arrives and break it down quietly after the last one leaves. An evening that closes at ten thirty is normal here, and it is staffed and priced as exactly that. Arrival, service hours, and breakdown are confirmed in writing before you commit.

Ice & Instinct / Westchester & Greenwich

#### An evening, held quietly.

Tell us the date, the town, and the guest count.
A tailored proposal returns within one business day.
